channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: New Uber feature may overcharge you. Here's how to turn it off
item.description: Unless you disable it, a new setting in the app may end up costing you the next time you hail a ride overseas.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KREX 5

item.title: Masses of mourners attend the funeral of Hezbollah leader Nasrallah, 5 months after his killing
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of people packed into a stadium in Beirut and nearby streets on Sunday for the funeral of Hezbollah's former leader, Hassan Nasrallah, nearly five months after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, ABBY SEWELL and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump, Musk aren't the first to make deep cuts: Clinton-era initiative saved billions
item.description: DOGE isn't the first controversial cost-cutting push — the biggest effort to overhaul the federal government in modern history actually was 30 years ago under a Democratic administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis conscious but remains in critical condition after respiratory crisis
item.description: Pope Francis was conscious but still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen Sunday following a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, as he remains in critical condition with a complex lung infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Beware of text messages about unpaid tolls, FBI and state authorities warn
item.description: Drivers should be on the lookout for scam text messages warning of an unpaid road toll bill, according to recent warnings from authorities.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KREX 5

item.title: Musk gives all federal workers 48 hours to explain what they did last week or face consequences
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of federal workers have been given little more than 48 hours to explain what they accomplished over the last week, sparking confusion across key agencies as billionaire Elon Musk expands his drive to slash the size of federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: French aircraft carrier stages combat drills with Filipinos in disputed sea and visits Philippines
item.description: ABOARD THE CHARLES DE GAULLE, Philippines (AP) — France’s nuclear-powered aircraft carrier and accompanying warships were in the Philippines on Sunday after holding combat drills with Filipino forces in the disputed South China Sea in a show of firepower that would likely antagonize China.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ AND JOEAL CALUPITAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump and Musk aren't the first to make deep cuts. Clinton-era Reinventing Government saved billions
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A new administration swept into Washington and announced plans to shake it up, using corporate know-how and new technology to streamline the federal bureaucracy. It offered millions of government employees buyouts and slashed costs to balance the budget.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kamala Harris receives prestigious Chairman's prize at NAACP Image Awards
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former Vice President Kamala Harris stepped on the NAACP Image Awards stage Saturday night with a sobering message, calling the civil rights organization a pillar of the Black community and urging people to stay resilient and hold onto their faith during the tenure of President Do
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: States threaten fines and jail time for local officials who resist Trump's immigration crackdown
item.description: Republican lawmakers in some states are threatening local officials with lawsuits, fines and jail time if they resist President Donald Trump's crackdown on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Kramon And David A. Lieb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ohio bathroom law targeting transgender students has brought internal strife to some campuses
item.description: For some famously progressive colleges in Ohio, a new state law designed to keep transgender women from using women’s restrooms at schools is bringing a moment of soul-searching for students, alumni and administrators.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hawaii man freed after 30 years in prison visits mother's grave and ponders ubiquitous cellphones
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the first places Gordon Cordeiro visited when a judge ordered him released after spending 30 years in prison for a killing he always maintained he had nothing to do with was his mother’s Hawaii gravesite.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USPS honors beloved actress Betty White with commemorative stamp
item.description: The stamp features a digitally created portrait of White against a violet background with "bubbly spots that befit her sparkling personality.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Border Patrol agent whose death is tied to cultlike Zizians is buried with military honors
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A U.S. Border Patrol agent whose killing in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border has been tied to a cultlike group was buried with full military honors Saturday at a national cemetery in Minnesota. David Maland, 44, died Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Israel delays release of Palestinian prisoners, citing 'humiliating' handovers of hostages
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel says the release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners is delayed “until the release of the next hostages has been assured, and without the humiliating ceremonies” at handovers of Israeli captives in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, MOHAMMED JAHJOUH and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: One dead, one injured after shooting at New Mexico Air Force base
item.description: Kirtland Air Force base said the 377th Security Forces Squadron was called to an incident at approximately 2 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Watch live: Trump set to address CPAC
item.description: President Trump on Saturday afternoon will headline the final day of the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis is in critical condition after a long respiratory crisis, requiring oxygen at high flow
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in critical condition Saturday after he suffered a prolonged asthmatic respiratory crisis while being treated for pneumonia and a complex lung infection, the Vatican said. The 88-year-old pope, who remains conscious, received “high flows” of oxygen to help him breathe.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Warren Buffett offers Donald Trump some advice while celebrating Berkshire Hathaway's success
item.description: Warren Buffett is celebrating the successes of Berkshire Hathaway’s companies last year and in the 60 years since he took over a struggling New England textile company and began converting it into a massive conglomerate.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Video shows barefoot foster child with no coat left in snow, begging to go back inside
item.description: A man in Haskell, Oklahoma, heard a child nearby begging to go back inside. When he saw the little girl standing outside in record-breaking freezing temperatures and wearing only pajamas, he called 911.
item.dc:creator: Heather Holeman/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Roblox is 'hunting ground for child sex predators,' new lawsuit claims
item.description: The lawsuit was filed earlier this month on behalf of a 13-year-old boy who was an "avid user of Roblox and Discord.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KRON4

item.title: Former federal worker shares frustration on TikTok after losing job in less than 2 months
item.description: A federal worker who lost her job after being in her position for less than two months hopped on TikTok to tell her story as well as share information and resources to help other federal workers in the same position.
item.dc:creator: Ya-Marie Sesay, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Luigi Mangione draws crowd for first court hearing since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The defendant wore a bulletproof vest and shackles. A woman in the crowd wore a “Free Luigi" scarf. Outside, throngs of people cheered and chanted his name.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida woman who told insurance compant 'delay, deny, depose' has charge dropped
item.description: The charge against Lakeland woman accused of threatening her health insurance company was dropped on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: 83 percent of Americans disapprove of Trump's Jan. 6 pardons: Poll
item.description: The latest poll also shows that more than half of Americans also do not approve of Trump's handling of the economy or his management of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: US housing market worth nearly $50 trillion, over double a decade ago
item.description: The total value of all U.S. homes has doubled over the past decade. These cities saw the fastest growth last year.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: ICE, Homeland Security raid bakery, charge owners with harboring illegal immigrants
item.description: Bakery owners in Texas are facing felony charges after ICE and HSI arrested eight suspected illegal immigrants employed by the shop.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One-armed basketball player makes history with basket
item.description: When her high school basketball coach cut her from the team senior year, Baileigh Sinaman-Daniel refused to let that rejection, or the fact she was born with one arm, end her dream.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Grand jury recommends Alabama police department be abolished
item.description: The small Alabama city of Hanceville, located about 45 miles north of Birmingham, placed its entire police force on administrative leave a day after the grand jury recommendation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, ABC 4

item.title: Polar vortex makes much of US colder than Greenland, but warmth is coming. Then more cold
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The polar vortex hit its peak across much of America on Wednesday, with an icy grip that made Arctic Greenland seem like a toasty vacation spot in comparison. Even Mars has been warmer than North Dakota this week. But there's hope.
item.dc:creator: BY SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
